title: Gattermann and Gattermann-Koch Formylation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Gattermann and Gattermann-Koch formylation describing electrophilic introduction of formyl groups onto activated arenes using either CO/HCl with Lewis-acid carriers or HCN/Zn(CN)2-derived iminium equivalents under strongly Lewis-acidic conditions.
notes: Key concepts: arene formylation under Friedel-Crafts-like conditions, distinction between Gattermann-Koch and Gattermann variants, use of CO or cyanide surrogates, and limits imposed by substrate activation and harsh acidic media.

entities.notes: Named aromatic formylation family overview.
Carbon-monoxide-based formylation variant highlighted on the overview page.
Generic product class generated by electrophilic aromatic formylation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: electrophilic aromatic formylation of activated arenes
similar_existing.description_short: Classical aromatic formylation family using either carbon monoxide/hydrogen chloride or cyanide-derived formylation equivalents under Lewis-acidic conditions.

title: Glaser Coupling – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Glaser coupling describing oxidative homocoupling and heterocoupling of terminal alkynes to furnish conjugated diynes under copper-mediated aerobic conditions and related Hay, Eglinton, and palladium-assisted variants.
notes: Key concepts: copper acetylide formation, oxidative dimerization, macrocyclization of diynes, heterocoupling logic, and solvent/ligand effects that tune homocoupling versus cross-coupling outcomes.

entities.notes: Named oxidative alkyne-coupling family overview.
Copper(II)-acetate-mediated modification highlighted on the overview page.
CuCl/TMEDA/O2 variant for diyne formation shown on the overview page.

similar_existing.transformation_type: oxidative coupling of terminal alkynes to diynes
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidative alkyne-coupling family widely used for diyne synthesis, macrocyclization, and heterocoupling variants in complex-molecule synthesis.

similar_existing.transformation_type: metal-mediated coupling of organohalides to carbon-carbon bonds
similar_existing.description_short: Classical sodium-mediated coupling of organohalides used for dimerization, cross-coupling, and intramolecular ring closure of suitable dihalides.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd- or Ni-catalyzed cross-coupling of organozinc reagents with organic electrophiles
similar_existing.description_short: Organozinc-based cross-coupling method valued for high chemoselectivity and compatibility with sensitive functional groups.

similar_existing.transformation_type: low-valent titanium-mediated reductive coupling of carbonyl compounds to alkenes
similar_existing.description_short: Reductive olefination method that couples carbonyl compounds under low-valent titanium conditions and is especially powerful for macrocyclization.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd/Cu-catalyzed coupling of terminal alkynes with aryl or vinyl halides
similar_existing.description_short: Cross-coupling reaction joining terminal alkynes with aryl or vinyl electrophiles under palladium and copper catalysis.

title: Hajos-Parrish Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Hajos-Parrish reaction describing asymmetric intramolecular aldol cyclization of Michael adducts from 1,3-diones and enones under catalytic or stoichiometric amino-acid conditions to form bicyclic enones in high ee.
notes: Key concepts: proline-catalyzed Michael/aldol annulation, relation to the Wieland-Miescher ketone, amino-acid catalysis, dehydration to bicyclic enones, and mechanistic stereochemical models involving enamine or iminium activation.

entities.notes: Named asymmetric Robinson-annulation family overview.
Closely related bicyclic enone synthesis explicitly referenced on the overview page.
Michael-addition/intramolecular-aldol annulation logic underlying the Hajos-Parrish reaction family.

similar_existing.transformation_type: asymmetric intramolecular aldol annulation to bicyclic enones
similar_existing.description_short: Asymmetric amino-acid-catalyzed annulation that generates optically enriched bicyclic enones central to steroid and terpenoid synthesis.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-catalyzed coupling of alkenes with aldehydes to form oxocarbenium-derived oxygen heterocycles and alcohols
similar_existing.description_short: Versatile acid-promoted alkene–aldehyde coupling that channels oxocarbenium chemistry into tetrahydropyrans, 1,3-dioxanes, allylic alcohols, and related oxygenated products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted condensation of aromatic aldehydes with acid anhydrides or azlactone precursors to α,β-unsaturated acids or azlactones
similar_existing.description_short: Classical olefination manifold that converts aromatic aldehydes into cinnamic-acid-type products or azlactone intermediates using anhydride-based carbon nucleophiles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-promoted cyclocondensation of aromatic carbonyl compounds with 2,2-dialkoxyethylamines to form isoquinolines or tetrahydroisoquinolines
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational isoquinoline-forming annulation that converts aromatic carbonyl compounds and aminoacetals into isoquinoline frameworks and supports multiple modified routes for complex alkaloid synthesis.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alkene -> cyclopropane
similar_existing.description_short: Color-explanation page family seed from page xiv.

title: Hantzsch Dihydropyridine Synthesis – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Hantzsch dihydropyridine synthesis describing multicomponent condensation of beta-keto esters or 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds with aldehydes and ammonia to furnish 1,4-dihydropyridines and, after oxidation, substituted pyridines.
notes: Key concepts: enamine and Knoevenagel-type intermediates, one-pot three-component assembly, oxidation of 1,4-dihydropyridines to pyridines, and scope with aldehydes, 1,3-dicarbonyl partners, and nitrogen sources.

entities.notes: Named multicomponent dihydropyridine-forming family overview.
Core product class produced by Hantzsch condensation before oxidation.
Oxidized aromatic heterocycle derived from Hantzsch dihydropyridines.

similar_existing.transformation_type: multicomponent condensation to 1,4-dihydropyridines and pyridines
similar_existing.description_short: Classical multicomponent condensation used to access 1,4-dihydropyridines and, after oxidation, substituted pyridines.

similar_existing.transformation_type: annulation of acylmethylpyridinium salts with alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones and ammonia equivalents to substituted pyridines
similar_existing.description_short: Pyridine-annulation method that converts acylmethylpyridinium salts and enones into substituted pyridines through Michael addition, diketone formation, and cyclodehydration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: annulative synthesis of 5-hydroxyindoles from benzoquinones and enamine partners
similar_existing.description_short: Benzoquinone-based indole annulation that furnishes substituted 5-hydroxyindoles from amino-enone or enamine partners.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-catalyzed indolization of arylhydrazones
similar_existing.description_short: Acid-catalyzed conversion of arylhydrazones into substituted indoles through tautomerization, sigmatropic rearrangement, and ring closure.

similar_existing.transformation_type: synthesis
similar_existing.description_short: Named reaction family listed in the alphabetical reaction index pages of the source book.

title: Heck Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Heck reaction describing palladium-catalyzed arylation or alkenylation of olefins using aryl, vinyl, or related halides or pseudohalides under basic conditions to generate substituted alkenes.
notes: Key concepts: oxidative addition, migratory insertion, beta-hydride elimination, ligand and base effects, intra- versus intermolecular Heck coupling, and broad use in C-C bond formation.

entities.notes: Named palladium-catalyzed olefin arylation family overview.
Alternative name explicitly associated with the Heck reaction family.
Generic product class furnished by Heck coupling of unsaturated partners.

similar_existing.transformation_type: palladium-catalyzed arylation or alkenylation of olefins
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational palladium-catalyzed coupling of unsaturated halides or pseudohalides with olefins to form substituted alkenes.

similar_existing.transformation_type: iodide-mediated rearrangement or ring expansion of activated aziridines
similar_existing.description_short: Aziridine rearrangement family that converts N-acylaziridines into oxazolines and related heterocycles under nucleophilic conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: nitroaldol carbon-carbon bond formation
similar_existing.description_short: Carbon-carbon bond-forming addition of nitroalkanes to carbonyl compounds to furnish beta-nitro alcohols and related products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: conversion of nitro compounds or nitronates into carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Classic nitro-to-carbonyl transformation that converts nitronate derivatives into aldehydes or ketones under acidic or related conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: aldehyde or ketone to α-amino nitrile
similar_existing.description_short: Classical and asymmetric cyanide addition to imines generated from carbonyl compounds and amines to access α-amino nitriles and amino acid precursors.

title: Heine Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Heine reaction describing nucleophile-promoted ring expansion or rearrangement of N-acylaziridines to 2-aryl-2-oxazolines, thiazolines, triazolines, and related heterocycles, typically under iodide-mediated conditions.
notes: Key concepts: iodide opening of activated aziridines, double inversion or net-retention stereochemical outcomes, solvent effects, and extension of the method to heteroatom-substituted aziridines.

entities.notes: Named aziridine rearrangement/ring-expansion family overview.
Five-membered heterocycle product class obtained from N-acylaziridines under Heine conditions.
Activated aziridine substrate class that undergoes nucleophile-promoted rearrangement.

similar_existing.transformation_type: iodide-mediated rearrangement or ring expansion of activated aziridines
similar_existing.description_short: Aziridine rearrangement family that converts N-acylaziridines into oxazolines and related heterocycles under nucleophilic conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: nitroaldol carbon-carbon bond formation
similar_existing.description_short: Carbon-carbon bond-forming addition of nitroalkanes to carbonyl compounds to furnish beta-nitro alcohols and related products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: conversion of nitro compounds or nitronates into carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Classic nitro-to-carbonyl transformation that converts nitronate derivatives into aldehydes or ketones under acidic or related conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted condensation of aromatic aldehydes with acid anhydrides or azlactone precursors to α,β-unsaturated acids or azlactones
similar_existing.description_short: Classical olefination manifold that converts aromatic aldehydes into cinnamic-acid-type products or azlactone intermediates using anhydride-based carbon nucleophiles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-mediated conversion of nitriles and alcohols or thiols into imino ether or imino thioether salts and downstream amidines or esters
similar_existing.description_short: Classical nitrile activation method that turns nitriles into imino ether salts and provides a versatile gateway to amidines, esters, and related nitrogen-rich motifs.

title: Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky reaction describing alpha-halogenation of carboxylic acids via in situ formation of acyl halides in the presence of halogen and catalytic or stoichiometric phosphorus reagents, followed by hydrolysis or nucleophilic trapping.
notes: Key concepts: conversion of acids to acyl halides, enol halogenation, hydrolysis back to alpha-halo acids, and interception with nucleophiles to give alpha-substituted derivatives.

entities.notes: Named alpha-halogenation family overview.
Common abbreviation and alternate short name for the Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky reaction.
Generic product class obtained by alpha-halogenation of carboxylic acids.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alpha-halogenation of carboxylic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Classical alpha-halogenation chemistry of carboxylic acids proceeding through in situ generated acyl halides.

similar_existing.transformation_type: metal-mediated coupling of α-halo esters with carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Classic zinc-based carbon–carbon bond-forming reaction between α-halo esters and carbonyl compounds that delivers β-hydroxy esters with broad modern metal-mediated variants.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Lewis-acid-mediated propargylic substitution/decomplexation of cobalt-alkyne complexes
similar_existing.description_short: Cobalt-complex-mediated propargylic substitution method that enables controlled generation and trapping of stabilized propargylic cations.

similar_existing.transformation_type: anti dihydroxylation of alkenes via halonium opening and carboxylate displacement
similar_existing.description_short: Classical vicinal dihydroxylation manifold that uses iodine and silver carboxylates to convert alkenes into stereodefined 1,2-diols under either dry or wet conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: iodide-mediated rearrangement or ring expansion of activated aziridines
similar_existing.description_short: Aziridine rearrangement family that converts N-acylaziridines into oxazolines and related heterocycles under nucleophilic conditions.

title: Henry Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Henry reaction describing base-promoted addition of nitroalkanes to aldehydes or ketones to afford beta-nitro alcohols that can be transformed into nitroalkenes, alpha-nitro ketones, or beta-amino alcohols.
notes: Key concepts: deprotonation of nitroalkanes, nitronate addition to carbonyls, reversibility, dehydration and redox transformations of beta-nitro alcohols, and asymmetric variants of nitroaldol chemistry.

entities.notes: Named nitroaldol family overview.
Common synonym for the Henry reaction explicitly referenced on the overview page.
Generic product class formed by nitroalkane addition to aldehydes or ketones.

similar_existing.transformation_type: nitroaldol carbon-carbon bond formation
similar_existing.description_short: Carbon-carbon bond-forming addition of nitroalkanes to carbonyl compounds to furnish beta-nitro alcohols and related products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: iodide-mediated rearrangement or ring expansion of activated aziridines
similar_existing.description_short: Aziridine rearrangement family that converts N-acylaziridines into oxazolines and related heterocycles under nucleophilic conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: palladium-catalyzed arylation or alkenylation of olefins
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational palladium-catalyzed coupling of unsaturated halides or pseudohalides with olefins to form substituted alkenes.

similar_existing.transformation_type: other
similar_existing.description_short: The Buchner reaction, involving the thermal or photochemical reaction of ethyl diazoacetate with benzene to form a cycloheptatriene derivative via a norcaradiene intermediate.

similar_existing.transformation_type: conversion of nitro compounds or nitronates into carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Classic nitro-to-carbonyl transformation that converts nitronate derivatives into aldehydes or ketones under acidic or related conditions.

title: Hofmann Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Hofmann rearrangement describing conversion of primary carboxamides into amines or trapped isocyanate derivatives through N-haloamide formation and migration to an isocyanate.
notes: Key concepts: N-haloamide intermediates, concerted rearrangement to isocyanates, retention of configuration, hydrolysis to one-carbon-shorter amines, and trapping of isocyanates with alcohols or amines.

entities.notes: Named amide-contraction/rearrangement family overview.
Central migrating intermediate formed from N-haloamides before hydrolysis or trapping.
Common trapped isocyanate-derived product class highlighted on the overview page.

similar_existing.transformation_type: amide contraction to amines via isocyanates
similar_existing.description_short: One-carbon contraction of primary amides to amines or isocyanate-derived products under hypohalite or hypervalent-iodine conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: allylic trichloroacetimidate to allylic trichloroacetamide [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement
similar_existing.description_short: Claisen-type allylic amination method that rearranges allylic trichloroacetimidates into allylic trichloroacetamides with predictable transfer of stereochemistry.

similar_existing.transformation_type: oxime to amide or lactam under acid activation with anti migration
similar_existing.description_short: The Beckmann rearrangement converts oximes into amides or lactams through activation of the oxime and anti migratory rearrangement via nitrilium ions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-catalyzed migration of a 2,3-epoxy alcohol to an isomeric epoxide; aza/thia variants form aziridino or thiirano alcohols
similar_existing.description_short: Epoxide migration manifold that relays stereochemistry and functional handles across epoxy alcohols and related heteroatom-substituted systems.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement of alpha-diazoketones to ketenes
similar_existing.description_short: Conversion of alpha-diazoketones into ketenes with accompanying 1,2-shift, widely used for ring contraction and homologation chemistry.

title: JOHNSON-CLAISEN REARRANGEMENT
summary: Overview page for the Johnson-Claisen rearrangement describing heating allylic alcohols with trialkyl orthoacetates under weakly acidic conditions to generate ketene acetals that undergo a [3,3]-sigmatropic shift to gamma,delta-unsaturated esters.
notes: Key concepts: exchange of allylic alcohols with ortho esters, ketene acetal formation, Claisen-type rearrangement under thermal or clay/microwave activation, and diastereocontrol explained by chairlike transition states with 1,3-diaxial interactions.

entities.notes: Named orthoester Claisen family overview.
Parent [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement family from which the Johnson-Claisen variant derives.
Characteristic rearrangement product class generated from allylic alcohols and ortho esters.

similar_existing.transformation_type: thermal Claisen rearrangement of allylic alcohols to gamma,delta-unsaturated esters
similar_existing.description_short: Claisen-type rearrangement of allylic alcohols and ortho esters that installs gamma,delta-unsaturated ester motifs with predictable alkene geometry.

similar_existing.transformation_type: allyl ether to allyl carbonyl or allyl phenol [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: allyl vinyl ether|allyl aryl ether|heat|Lewis acid variants
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allyl vinyl and allyl aryl ethers to gamma,delta-unsaturated carbonyl compounds or allyl phenols.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: [3,3] | 1. LHMDS, toluene, -78 °C; 2. 120 °C, 6h | CH3CH2COCl; K2CO3, Me3Al; CHCl3, 0 °C
similar_existing.description_short: Amide enolate induced aza-Claisen rearrangement in the synthesis of fluvircinine A1

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement of activated hydroxamic acids to isocyanates and trapped amine/urea/carbamate products
similar_existing.description_short: Activated-hydroxamate rearrangement that furnishes isocyanates under mild conditions and enables access to amines, carbamates, and ureas after trapping.

similar_existing.transformation_type: allylic alcohol to gamma,delta-unsaturated amide rearrangement
similar_existing.description_short: Claisen-type rearrangement that converts allylic alcohols into gamma,delta-unsaturated amides under thermal conditions using DMA-DMA.

family_name_norm: kagan molander samarium diiodide mediated coupling
title: KAGAN-MOLANDER SAMARIUM DIIODIDE-MEDIATED COUPLING
summary: Overview page for Kagan-Molander samarium diiodide-mediated coupling describing intermolecular and intramolecular SmI2-promoted reactions of alkyl halides or tosylates with aldehydes and ketones, together with acyl substitutions and tandem couplings to cyclic ketones or bicyclic alcohols.
notes: Key concepts: samarium Grignard and samarium Barbier manifolds, HMPA or DMPU co-solvent acceleration, NiI2/Fe(DBM)3/copper additives, nucleophilic acyl substitution of halo esters, and tandem coupling sequences that generate complex polycycles.

entities.notes: SmI2-mediated coupling family overview.
The overview explicitly situates Kagan-Molander chemistry within samarium Barbier and samarium Grignard manifolds.
Representative product class obtained from intramolecular SmI2-mediated coupling of halo carbonyl precursors.

similar_existing.transformation_type: SmI2-mediated coupling of alkyl halides or halo carbonyl derivatives with carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Samarium diiodide-based coupling platform that enables intermolecular and intramolecular Barbier, Grignard, Reformatsky, and acyl-substitution processes in complex synthesis.

title: KAHNE GLYCOSIDATION
summary: Overview page for the Kahne glycosidation describing activation of glycosyl sulfoxides with triflic anhydride or related activators to generate glycosyl triflates that react with O-, S-, or N-nucleophiles with high anomeric control.
notes: Key concepts: sulfoxide method; glycosyl triflate formation; activation by Tf2O, TMSOTf, or TfOH with bases such as DTBMP/TTBP; participation of neighboring groups; and low-temperature coupling to alcohol, phenol, thiol, or amide nucleophiles.

entities.notes: Sulfoxide-based glycosylation family overview.
Key reactive intermediate formed upon activation of glycosyl sulfoxides.
Classical glycosidation method contrasted with the Kahne sulfoxide strategy.

similar_existing.transformation_type: glycosylation via glycosyl sulfoxide activation to glycosyl triflates followed by stereocontrolled O-, S-, or N-glycoside formation
similar_existing.description_short: Sulfoxide-based glycosylation method that activates glycosyl sulfoxides to glycosyl triflates and couples them with hindered O- or N-nucleophiles with high stereocontrol.

similar_existing.transformation_type: glycosidation of glycosyl halides with alcohols or phenols under heavy-metal salts or Lewis-acid activation
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational glycosidation method using glycosyl halides and metal-salt or Lewis-acid promoters to form O-glycosides with substrate- and neighboring-group-controlled stereochemistry.

similar_existing.transformation_type: activated aromatic aldehyde or ketone to substituted phenol
similar_existing.description_short: Oxidation/rearrangement of activated aromatic aldehydes or ketones to substituted phenols through peroxide addition and aryl migration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: chromium(VI)-mediated oxidation of alcohols to carbonyl compounds or carboxylic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Chromium-based oxidation platform encompassing Jones reagent and related Cr(VI) reagents for the conversion of alcohols and related substrates into carbonyl compounds.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd/Cu-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes to carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes, especially terminal alkenes, to carbonyl compounds including methyl ketones and related oxidative cyclization products.

title: KECK MACROLACTONIZATION
summary: Overview page for the Keck macrolactonization describing high-dilution cyclization of hydroxy acids using carbodiimide activation with DMAP and DMAP·HCl or EDCI to form medium- and large-ring lactones.
notes: Key concepts: syringe-pump substrate addition under reflux, DCC or EDCI activation, DMAP/DMAP·HCl proton-transfer mediation, suppression of N-acylurea byproducts, and comparison with Corey-Nicolaou and Shiina-type macrolactonizations.

entities.notes: Macrolactonization family overview.
Earlier macrolactonization protocol explicitly contrasted with the Keck modification.
Characteristic macrocyclic product class generated from hydroxy-acid precursors.

similar_existing.transformation_type: intramolecular macrolactonization of hydroxy acids to medium- and large-ring lactones using carbodiimide activation with DMAP/DMAP.HCl or EDCI
similar_existing.description_short: Macrolactonization protocol for cyclizing hydroxy acids to macrocycles using carbodiimide activation and DMAP/DMAP.HCl mediation under high-dilution conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: high-dilution macrolactonization of hydroxy acids to medium or large ring lactones
similar_existing.description_short: Macrocyclization method that converts hydroxy acids into medium- and large-ring lactones via mixed anhydrides and DMAP-catalyzed intramolecular acyl transfer.

similar_existing.transformation_type: hydroxy acid cyclization to medium- or large-ring lactone
similar_existing.description_short: Macrolactonization method that activates hydroxy acids as 2-pyridinethiol esters before intramolecular closure to medium- and large-ring lactones.

similar_existing.transformation_type: radical allyl transfer from allyltributyltin to alkyl halides or related radical precursors
similar_existing.description_short: Free-radical allylation method that replaces halides or related radical leaving groups with allyl fragments using allyltributyltin under chain conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters to cyclic beta-keto esters
similar_existing.description_short: Intramolecular Claisen condensation of diesters that yields cyclic beta-keto esters, often complemented by related nitrile cyclizations in ring-building synthesis.

title: KECK RADICAL ALLYLATION
summary: Overview page for the Keck radical allylation describing free-radical substitution of alkyl halides and related radical precursors with allyltributyltin under AIBN, benzoyl peroxide, heat, or photolysis to install allyl groups.
notes: Key concepts: atom-transfer initiation from alkyl bromides, chlorides, or selenides; radical chain propagation with allyltributyltin; chemoselective allylation of halides over many functional groups; and mechanistic comparison with earlier Kosugi and Grignon procedures.

entities.notes: Radical allylation family overview.
Key allyl donor used in tin-mediated radical chain transfer.
Representative radical precursor class for allyl substitution under Keck conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: radical allyl transfer from allyltributyltin to alkyl halides or related radical precursors
similar_existing.description_short: Free-radical allylation method that replaces halides or related radical leaving groups with allyl fragments using allyltributyltin under chain conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: enantioselective allylation of aldehydes to homoallylic alcohols using allylstannanes or allylsilanes under chiral Lewis-acid catalysis
similar_existing.description_short: Catalytic enantioselective allylation platform that converts aldehydes into chiral homoallylic alcohols using allylstannanes and BINOL/Ti(IV) Lewis-acid catalysts.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Lewis-acid-promoted allylation of carbonyl compounds with allylsilanes
similar_existing.description_short: Carbon–carbon bond-forming allylation of carbonyl compounds using allylsilanes under Lewis-acid activation.

similar_existing.transformation_type: other
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: CCl3Br | BrC(Cl)(Cl)Cl | reagent; (PhSe)2 | c1ccc(SeSeC2=CC=CC=C2)cc1 | reagent | hν | reagent; stir, r.t. | reagent
similar_existing.description_short: Barton Reaction

similar_existing.transformation_type: palladium-catalyzed arylation or alkenylation of olefins
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational palladium-catalyzed coupling of unsaturated halides or pseudohalides with olefins to form substituted alkenes.

title: KNORR PYRROLE SYNTHESIS
summary: Overview page for the Knorr pyrrole synthesis describing condensation of alpha-amino ketones with ketones or active methylene compounds to furnish substituted pyrroles, often after in situ reduction of alpha-nitroso ketones.
notes: Key concepts: preparation of alpha-amino ketones from alpha-nitroso ketones under Zn/AcOH, sodium dithionite, or hydrogenation conditions; condensation with 1,3-dicarbonyl or other activated carbonyl partners; imine/enamine cyclization; and regiochemical control in substituted pyrrole formation.

entities.notes: Condensation-based pyrrole synthesis family overview.
Characteristic heteroaromatic product class generated from alpha-amino ketones and carbonyl partners.
Mentioned as an alternative route to the required alpha-amino ketone substrate class.

similar_existing.transformation_type: condensation of alpha-amino ketones with ketones or active methylene compounds to substituted pyrroles
similar_existing.description_short: Classical pyrrole synthesis that converts alpha-amino ketones and carbonyl partners into highly substituted pyrroles through condensation, enamine formation, and ring closure.

similar_existing.transformation_type: condensation of 1,4-dicarbonyl compounds with ammonia or primary amines to substituted pyrroles
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational pyrrole-forming condensation that converts 1,4-dicarbonyl compounds and ammonia equivalents or amines into substituted pyrroles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: cyclodehydration of 1,4-dicarbonyl compounds or surrogates to substituted furans
similar_existing.description_short: Classical heterocycle-forming reaction that transforms 1,4-dicarbonyl systems into furans by acid- or dehydrating-agent-mediated cyclodehydration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: annulation of acylmethylpyridinium salts with alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones and ammonia equivalents to substituted pyridines
similar_existing.description_short: Pyridine-annulation method that converts acylmethylpyridinium salts and enones into substituted pyridines through Michael addition, diketone formation, and cyclodehydration.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd-catalyzed heteroannulation of o-iodoanilines with internal alkynes to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed annulation of o-iodoanilines with alkynes that provides substituted indoles and fused azaindole-type scaffolds with predictable regioselectivity.

title: LIEBEN HALOFORM REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Lieben haloform reaction describing base-promoted halogenation and cleavage of methyl ketones or methyl carbinols to haloforms and carboxylates.
notes: Key concepts: repeated alpha-halogenation of methyl ketones, formation and hydrolysis of trihalomethyl ketones, use of hypohalite or halogen/base combinations, and application as a one-carbon degradation strategy for methyl ketone functionality.

entities.notes: Haloform-reaction family overview.
Key over-halogenated intermediate that undergoes hydrolysis and C-C bond cleavage in the haloform reaction.
Characteristic CHX3 byproduct released during conversion of methyl ketones into carboxylates.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted halogenation and cleavage of methyl ketones or methyl carbinols to haloforms and carboxylates
similar_existing.description_short: One-carbon degradation method that converts methyl ketones and related methyl carbinols into haloforms and carboxylate products under hypohalite conditions.

similar_existing.transformation_type: auxiliary-controlled syn-selective aldol addition
similar_existing.description_short: Highly diastereoselective syn aldol reaction of chiral N-acyl oxazolidinones with aldehydes through Z-boron enolates and Zimmerman-Traxler transition states.

similar_existing.transformation_type: nitrile-based aromatic acylation to aryl ketones
similar_existing.description_short: Electrophilic aromatic acylation that converts activated nitriles and electron-rich arenes into aromatic ketones after hydrolysis.

similar_existing.transformation_type: carboxylation of phenoxide salts with carbon dioxide to salicylic-acid-type aromatic hydroxy acids
similar_existing.description_short: Aromatic carboxylation method that transforms phenoxides into salicylic-acid or para-hydroxybenzoic-acid derivatives under CO2, heat, and base.

similar_existing.transformation_type: metal-mediated coupling of α-halo esters with carbonyl compounds
similar_existing.description_short: Classic zinc-based carbon–carbon bond-forming reaction between α-halo esters and carbonyl compounds that delivers β-hydroxy esters with broad modern metal-mediated variants.

title: LOSSEN REARRANGEMENT
summary: Overview page for the Lossen rearrangement describing conversion of O-acyl or O-aryl hydroxamic acids into isocyanates that are trapped by water, alcohols, or amines to give amines, carbamates, or ureas.
notes: Key concepts: activation of hydroxamic acids by acylation or related derivatization, base- or heat-induced rearrangement to isocyanates, close relationship to Curtius and Hofmann rearrangements, and utility under relatively mild conditions without strong dehydrating reagents.

entities.notes: Hydroxamate-to-isocyanate rearrangement family overview.
Activated hydroxamic-acid derivative that undergoes rearrangement under basic or thermal conditions.
Transient rearrangement product trapped by water, alcohols, or amines to furnish amines, carbamates, or ureas.

similar_existing.transformation_type: rearrangement of activated hydroxamic acids to isocyanates and trapped amine/urea/carbamate products
similar_existing.description_short: Activated-hydroxamate rearrangement that furnishes isocyanates under mild conditions and enables access to amines, carbamates, and ureas after trapping.

similar_existing.transformation_type: allyl ether to allyl carbonyl or allyl phenol [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: allyl vinyl ether|allyl aryl ether|heat|Lewis acid variants
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allyl vinyl and allyl aryl ethers to gamma,delta-unsaturated carbonyl compounds or allyl phenols.

similar_existing.transformation_type: 1,5-diene [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement / ring expansion of strained dienes
similar_existing.description_short: Thermal reversible [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of 1,5-dienes that reorganizes carbon skeletons, including medium-ring-forming strained-substrate variants.

similar_existing.transformation_type: allylic trichloroacetimidate to allylic trichloroacetamide [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement
similar_existing.description_short: Claisen-type allylic amination method that rearranges allylic trichloroacetimidates into allylic trichloroacetamides with predictable transfer of stereochemistry.

similar_existing.transformation_type: [1,2]-migration of ammonium or sulfonium ylides
similar_existing.description_short: Base-promoted rearrangement of ammonium and sulfonium ylides that transfers carbon substituents adjacent to heteroatom centers.

title: LUCHE REDUCTION
summary: Overview page for the Luche reduction describing selective 1,2-reduction of alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones to allylic alcohols with NaBH4 in the presence of cerium(III) chloride.
notes: Key concepts: in situ formation of alkoxyborohydride species, Lewis-acid activation of enones by CeCl3, suppression of conjugate reduction, fast room-temperature operation in methanol or related alcohol solvents, and high chemoselectivity toward enones over isolated carbonyls.

entities.notes: Cerium-assisted 1,2-reduction family overview.
Characteristic enone substrate that is reduced selectively at the carbonyl carbon under Luche conditions.
Signature 1,2-reduction product produced from acyclic or cyclic enones using CeCl3/NaBH4.

similar_existing.transformation_type: CeCl3/NaBH4 reduction of alpha,beta-unsaturated ketones to allylic alcohols
similar_existing.description_short: Selective 1,2-reduction of enones to allylic alcohols using sodium borohydride in the presence of cerium(III) chloride.

similar_existing.transformation_type: dissolving-metal dearomatization / reductive alkylation
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: Li or Na, NH3(l), ROH, t-BuOH, EtOH
similar_existing.description_short: Dissolving-metal reduction of aromatic rings and heteroarenes to unconjugated dienes or partially reduced heterocycles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: other
similar_existing.description_short: The Buchner reaction, involving the thermal or photochemical reaction of ethyl diazoacetate with benzene to form a cycloheptatriene derivative via a norcaradiene intermediate.

similar_existing.transformation_type: palladium-catalyzed arylation or alkenylation of olefins
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational palladium-catalyzed coupling of unsaturated halides or pseudohalides with olefins to form substituted alkenes.

similar_existing.transformation_type: iodide-mediated rearrangement or ring expansion of activated aziridines
similar_existing.description_short: Aziridine rearrangement family that converts N-acylaziridines into oxazolines and related heterocycles under nucleophilic conditions.

title: MADELUNG INDOLE SYNTHESIS
summary: Overview page for the Madelung indole synthesis describing base-promoted cyclization of N-acyl-o-toluidines or related anilides to 2-substituted indoles, including Smith-modified low-temperature variants.
notes: Key concepts: strong-base deprotonation alpha to the amide, intramolecular cyclization onto the acyl carbonyl, high-temperature classical conditions with alkoxides or sodamide, and modern alkynyllithium/Peterson-olefination variants that operate under milder conditions.

entities.notes: Classical and modified Madelung cyclization overview.
Canonical anilide precursor that cyclizes under strongly basic conditions to form indoles.
Characteristic indole product class produced by classical or Smith-modified Madelung cyclizations.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted cyclization of N-acyl-o-toluidines or related anilides to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Classical and modified intramolecular anilide cyclization methods for building 2-substituted and 2,3-disubstituted indoles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: substituted nitroarene or nitrosoarene to indole using excess vinyl or alkenyl Grignard reagent
similar_existing.key_reagents_clue: vinylmagnesium bromide|alkenyl Grignard|THF|-40 to -78 C|acidic work-up|nitroarene
similar_existing.description_short: The Bartoli indole synthesis rapidly converts ortho-substituted nitroarenes into indoles or azaindoles using excess vinyl or alkenyl Grignard reagents.

similar_existing.transformation_type: Pd-catalyzed heteroannulation of o-iodoanilines with internal alkynes to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Palladium-catalyzed annulation of o-iodoanilines with alkynes that provides substituted indoles and fused azaindole-type scaffolds with predictable regioselectivity.

similar_existing.transformation_type: acid-catalyzed indolization of arylhydrazones
similar_existing.description_short: Acid-catalyzed conversion of arylhydrazones into substituted indoles through tautomerization, sigmatropic rearrangement, and ring closure.

similar_existing.transformation_type: annulative synthesis of 5-hydroxyindoles from benzoquinones and enamine partners
similar_existing.description_short: Benzoquinone-based indole annulation that furnishes substituted 5-hydroxyindoles from amino-enone or enamine partners.

title: MALONIC ESTER SYNTHESIS
summary: Overview page for the malonic ester synthesis describing mono- and dialkylation of malonate esters followed by hydrolysis and decarboxylation to substituted acetic acids.
notes: Key concepts: generation of enolates from malonic esters, SN2 alkylation with primary electrophiles, optional dialkylation or cyclization, and hydrolytic decarboxylation to mono- or disubstituted acetic-acid products.

entities.notes: Active-methylene alkylation family overview.
Foundational active-methylene substrate that undergoes mono- or dialkylation after deprotonation.
Characteristic decarboxylation product obtained after alkylation and hydrolysis of malonate esters.

similar_existing.transformation_type: alkylation of malonic esters followed by hydrolysis and decarboxylation to substituted acetic acids
similar_existing.description_short: Foundational active-methylene strategy that converts alkylated malonate esters into substituted acetic acids and related cyclic products.

similar_existing.transformation_type: β-keto ester alkylation and decarboxylative ketone synthesis
similar_existing.description_short: Classical acetoacetic ester synthesis uses C-alkylation of acetoacetic esters followed by hydrolysis and decarboxylation to furnish mono- or disubstituted ketones.

similar_existing.transformation_type: SN2 ether formation from alkoxides and alkyl electrophiles
similar_existing.description_short: Formation of ethers by SN2 reaction of alkoxides or phenoxides with alkyl electrophiles, including intra- and intermolecular variants.

similar_existing.transformation_type: base-promoted cyclization of N-acyl-o-toluidines or related anilides to indoles
similar_existing.description_short: Classical and modified intramolecular anilide cyclization methods for building 2-substituted and 2,3-disubstituted indoles.

similar_existing.transformation_type: functionalization
similar_existing.description_short: Formation of a mixed anhydride from an alpha-amino acid
